user_idea: A retired father wants to meet his daughter one last time before she leaves Vietnam.

story_plan.core_idea: A proud retired father races across the city to say one honest goodbye to his daughter before she emigrates.
story_plan.protagonist: Minh, a retired bus mechanic who expresses love through practical help instead of words.
story_plan.main_conflict: Minh wants to stop his daughter from leaving but slowly realizes what she needs is his blessing, not his permission.
story_plan.emotional_goal: Move from control and regret toward honest acceptance.
story_plan.theme: Loving someone can mean releasing them without making them carry your fear.
story_plan.beginning: Minh finds out his daughter Linh is leaving for the airport earlier than expected and notices she left behind a childhood keychain.
story_plan.middle: He crosses familiar parts of the city, trying to reach her while rehearsing excuses instead of the apology he owes.
story_plan.ending: At the terminal curb, Minh gives Linh the keychain and says he is proud of her, letting the goodbye stay simple.
key_scenes: Minh repairs an old fan in silence while listening to Linh's voice message.
A taxi ride forces him to admit to the driver that he does not know how to apologize.
At the airport curb, Minh chooses not to ask Linh to stay.
visual_motifs: Hands fixing objects instead of touching
story_plan.production_notes: Keep locations limited to Minh's apartment, a taxi, and the airport exterior or bus station substitute.

strengths: Clear emotional stakes built around a final goodbye.
Small cast and limited locations make the story filmable.
The keychain gives the father a visual way to express love.
weaknesses: The daughter's own desire may need more specificity.
The middle could become repetitive if every beat is only travel.
suggestions: Give Linh one line or action that shows why leaving matters to her.
Make each travel beat reveal a different layer of Minh's avoidance.
End on a quiet image rather than a long speech.
